events.text: {"decision":"fail","risk":"high","work_stage":"in-service","component_conditions":["non-compliant","failed"],"percent_complete":100,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.9,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["T-tapping on a Class B fire alarm circuit violates NFPA 72 supervision requirements, creating an unsupervised branch that can lose devices without a trouble signal","Class B circuits require a single path with end-of-line supervision; T-taps bypass supervision and can leave devices inoperative without indication at the panel"],"actions":["Remove all T-taps and rewire Class B circuit as a continuous loop with proper end-of-line device","Test and verify supervision and device operation after correction","Document correction and notify AHJ/third-party inspector for re-inspection"],"rationale":"T-tapping a Class B fire alarm circuit breaks the supervised path required by NFPA 72, allowing devices to be lost without a trouble signal; this is a non-compliant installation that must fail.","references":["NFPA 72"]}
